channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ICC prosecution asserts readiness to establish witness credibility in Duterte case
item.description: Sinabi ni lawyer Gilbert Andres na handa ang prosecution ng International Criminal Court (ICC) na patunayan ang kredibilidad ng mga testigo laban kay dating Pangulong Rodrigo Duterte kaugnay ng imbest...

item.title: Rahul Singh's debut novel 'Unfolding' explores masculinity, queer relationships, and identity
item.description: Rahul Singh’s debut novel, 'Unfolding', offers a poignant exploration of masculinity, relationships, and identity through its protagonists—Rahul, Ojas, and Zubina. The narrative delves into the comple...

item.title: Woman and young child tragically die in water incident at Elthorne Park, Ealing
item.description: A tragic incident occurred at Elthorne Park in Ealing, west London, on Saturday afternoon, resulting in the deaths of a woman and a young child, believed to be mother and son. The Metropolitan Police ...

item.title: Clare Rigden’s TV Roundup: New Comedy and Drama Highlights on ABC, Netflix and SBS
item.description: Clare Rigden’s latest TV reviews dive into a mix of comedies, dramas, and quirky shows. She kicks off with the new ABC comedy *Bad Company*, starring Anne Edmonds and Kitty Flanagan, which portrays th...

item.title: Fire destroys bungalow in Lugbe, no casualties reported
item.description: A fire outbreak on Saturday evening completely destroyed a bungalow in the Federal Housing Authority (FHA) estate located in Lugbe, along Airport Road, Abuja. The incident occurred around 7:30 p.m., r...

item.title: Kharishma: Limpopo Artist Wins Best New Artist at Metro FM Awards 2026
item.description: Kharishma, born Melita Mogale, has emerged as a major force in South African music after winning the Best New Artist award at the 2026 Metro FM Music Awards. Hailing from Limpopo, she is known as the ...

item.title: Manchester City Qualify for FA Cup Final After Dramatic Win
item.description: Manchester City don secure their place for the FA Cup final after a nail-biting semi-final match against Southampton. Things look bleak for City when Finn Azaz scored a late goal for Southampton, putt...

item.title: Local elections likely to reflect voter dissatisfaction with national leadership
item.description: The article argues that the upcoming local and devolved elections in the UK are likely to be heavily influenced by public sentiment towards national political leadership, particularly Prime Minister S...

item.title: Congress to challenge Gauhati High Court decision denying anticipatory bail to Pawan Khera in Supreme Court
item.description: The Indian National Congress has expressed strong support for its senior leader Pawan Khera after the Gauhati High Court declined to grant him anticipatory bail in a case lodged by the Assam Police. T...

item.title: Centenarian Roy Allen to Walk 5K at Ottawa Race Weekend for Seniors' Causes
item.description: Roy Allen, a 100-year-old from Winnipeg, will make history next month by becoming the first centenarian to complete a 5K during the Tamarack Ottawa Race Weekend. Allen aims to raise funds for seniors'...

item.title: Nigeria’s 10 states that have produced presidents since independence
item.description: Since Nigeria gained independence on October 1, 1960, only 10 out of the 36 states plus the Federal Capital Territory have produced a head of state or president, either military or civilian. Across si...

item.title: Sudbury youth suffers serious injuries after pickup truck accident
item.description: A young person in Sudbury, Northern Ontario, sustained serious injuries following a collision with a pickup truck. The incident occurred earlier today, and local authorities responded quickly to the s...

item.title: Eze’s Goal Gives Arsenal Crucial Win Over Newcastle to Reclaim League Lead
item.description: Arsenal don return to the top of the Premier League after dem grind out a tight 1-0 win against Newcastle for Emirates Stadium, thanks to one sharp goal from Eberechi Eze. The win come at a very impor...

item.title: Security guard arrested after stealing gold and cash and using part of it to start a business
item.description: A private security guard working for Magnum Security Company, identified as Akena, was arrested in Uganda after allegedly stealing a bag containing both cash and gold. The incident happened on April 1...

item.title: Assessing Kitchener’s Drug Crisis One Year After Supervised Consumption Site Closure
item.description: One year after Kitchener’s supervised drug-use site closed, the city continues to grapple with the opioid crisis. While overdose rates have declined slightly, new and dangerous substances, including s...

item.title: U.S. tariffs force indefinite shutdown of Northern Ontario sawmills, impacting forestry jobs
item.description: Several sawmills in Northern Ontario have been shut down indefinitely as a direct result of ongoing U.S. tariffs on Canadian lumber, dealing a significant blow to the region’s forestry sector. The clo...

item.title: Fatal crash near Yass claims the life of a woman, several others injured
item.description: A tragic crash on the Hume Highway near Yass on Saturday afternoon resulted in the death of a 69-year-old woman. The incident occurred at the intersection of the Hume Highway and Yass Valley Way, in M...

item.title: Manchester City overcomes Southampton in FA Cup semi-final with late winner
item.description: In a dramatic FA Cup semi-final, Manchester City staged a thrilling comeback to defeat Southampton and reach a historic fourth consecutive final. Southampton initially took the lead in the 79th minute...

item.title: Manchester City stage late comeback against Southampton to secure fourth consecutive FA Cup final spot
item.description: Manchester City produced a late comeback to defeat Championship side Southampton 2-1 at Wembley, booking their place in a historic fourth consecutive FA Cup final. Despite being strong favourites, Cit...

item.title: Arviat selected as site for Canada’s first Inuit-led university, set to open in 2030
item.description: The hamlet of Arviat, Nunavut, has been selected as the site for Inuit Nunangat University (INU), marking a historic milestone as Canada’s first Inuit-led university and the first university in the Ar...
